free_text: Determination of the 9-Be(A,N)12-C Reaction Rate
The Berillium Targets were Solid Metallic Targets.
The Berillium has Been Evaporated on a Copper Backing.
The Exact Determination of the Target Thickness has
Been done Using the Narrow Resonance at Alpha-Particle
Energy=620 KeV.
The Detector Consists of a Cylindrical
Polyethylene Moderator and a Matrix of 16 3-He-Filled
Proportional Counters.
See Sample
The Beam Energy has Been Calibrated Using
Graphite Targets and a Narrow Resonance at 1056.3-KeV
Of the Reaction C-13(A,N)O-16
The Data Tables were Received from the Authors.
From R.Kunz
Last checking has been done.
The Uncertainty is Shown in Data Tables Without
Detailed Analysis.
Analytical Expression of the Reaction Rate is
Given.
Astrophysical Factor
- By Authors: Because the 9-Be(A,N)12-C Yield is
Several Orders of Magnitude Larger Than the Yield from
The Backing, Neutrons Arising from Background Reactions
Could be Neglected.
- By Compiler:
1. With the 4-Pi Detector a Rough Energy Determination
Of the Neutron Energies Could be Performed Making Use
Of the Energy Dependence of Neutron Diffusion.
2. Van Der Zwan and Geiger (SEE NP/A,152,481,1970)
Showed That the Yield Contribution of the Second
Excited State of C-12 Group is Rather Small For
Energies <3.7 MeV, Therefore the Group was not
Considered in this Investigation.
- By Author: Total Width is Given in C.-M. System
The Uncertainty is Given in Data Table
Without any Analysis.
- By Author: the Strength of the Resonance is Given in
C.-M.  System
The Uncertainty is Given in Data Table
Without any Analysis.
